articles.title: Germany says US troop withdrawal 'foreseeable' as Nato seeks clarification
articles.summary: German Defence Minister Boris Pistorius said the US decision to withdraw 5,000 troops was to be expected.

articles.title: US threatens shipping firms with sanctions if they pay Iran tolls
articles.summary: The warning comes as President Donald Trump said he was "not excited" by Iran's latest proposal for a peace deal.

articles.title: Thirteen killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, health ministry says
articles.summary: Four women and a child are among the dead, as fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah continues despite a ceasefire.

articles.title: 'If we sleep, they bite': Rats and weasels infest camps for displaced Gazans
articles.summary: In the Gaza Strip, the daily battles are now with rats, weasels, and other pests spreading diseases.

articles.title: Ex-F1 driver and Paralympic champion Zanardi dies
articles.summary: Former Formula 1 driver and Paralympic champion Alex Zanardi dies at the age of 59.

articles.title: Spirit Airlines shutting down after rescue talks collapse
articles.summary: The airline had been in talks with the Trump administration about a $500m bailout.

articles.title: California to begin ticketing driverless cars that violate traffic laws
articles.summary: Under the new rules, police will be able to issue tickets directly to the car's manufacturer when an autonomous vehicle breaks a traffic law.

articles.title: Cuba condemns new US sanctions as 'illegal' and 'abusive'
articles.summary: The new measures come on top of a US blockade of oil to Cuba that has caused widespread blackouts and fuel shortages.

articles.title: US court limits mail-order access to abortion pill mifepristone
articles.summary: The court decision curbs access to the drug used in medication abortions, which is currently the most common method for the procedure in the US.

articles.title: Trump tells Congress ceasefire means he does not need their approval for Iran war
articles.summary: The president writes that hostilities "have terminated" because of the ceasefire, arguing he does not need congressional authorisation.
